title: The Annotated Brief
setup:
Day three. Leo materialises next to your desk with a printed document — dense, highlighted, three colours of annotation in margins you can barely read. He sets it down like he's returning a library book.

'Previous associate left it on the shared drive. I cleaned it up a bit. Thought it might be useful for the Q4 presentation — there's context in there that isn't in the Loom brief.'

He's already half-turned to leave. You have approximately six seconds to say something before he goes back to his screen.

options.label: Accept it
options.text: You thank him and take it. That's a document that would take you a week to compile on your own. You don't say that. Leo nods and returns to his desk without making anything of it.
options.innerMonologue: He didn't expect anything back. That makes it harder to know what you owe.

options.label: Accept and share with Zara
options.text: You take it and mention you'll pass it to Zara — that she'd probably find it useful too. Leo's expression does something small. He says nothing. 'Sure,' he says eventually.
options.innerMonologue: Leo gave it to you specifically. You redistributed it. You're not sure that was the same thing.

options.label: Decline
options.text: You thank him and say you'd rather find your own way in. He nods — not disapproving, genuinely unsurprised. 'Fair enough,' he says, and takes it back.
options.innerMonologue: You chose the harder version. You're not sure you chose it for the right reason.

options.label: Accept and ask what Leo took from it
options.text: You take the document, then ask him what the most useful thing in it was — not what's on the page, but what he got from reading it. He stops walking. He turns back.
options.innerMonologue: That question bought you ten minutes of Leo actually talking. You suspect that's rare.

designNotes: Leo's small favour fires here. The mechanical point is Leo relationship delta: options A and D produce the strongest Leo gains. Option B is the trap — it looks generous but Leo reads it as redistributing something personal. Option C is the integrity choice with real cost. The hidden Leo-political-reveal arc (y01q07) becomes richer if the player took D here and has Rel >=50 going in.
